rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY CHIAPELONE VS. STEVEN CHIAPELONE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause To Defendant Steven Chiapelone
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 23, 2019 line . Order To Show Cause To Defendant Steven Chiapelone OFF CALENDAR. No proof of service on file. Per July 25, 2019 Order, a proof of service was to be filed 10 days before the hearing. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ISIDORA G. CERALDE ET AL VS. SPECIALIZED LOAN SERVICING, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Partial Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 23, 2019 line . DEFENDANTs SPECIALIZED LOAN SERVICING, LLC., MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION SYSTEMS, and HSBC BANK USA, N.A.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Partial Summary Adjudication GRANTED. Defendant presents evidence that Plaintiff is not a party to the operative Notice and Deed of Trust, i.e. is not the borrower. In opposition Plaintiff does not dispute that a "borrower" status is necessary for standing and does not cite any authority for the proposition that she has been afforded this status after the fact, i.e. loss mitigation options in the context of bankruptcy." (Opposition 8:18 - 9:4). = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK A STATE CHARTERED BANK VS. RENKA PROP. LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order: (1) Compelling Further Responses To Plaintiff Umpqua Banks: (A) Demand For Inspection And Production Of Documents (Set One), And (B) Form Interrogatories (Set One) On Shortened Time Pursuant To This Courts Order Granting Ex Parte Application For Order On Motion For: (1) Terminating Sanctions, (2) Striking Defendants Answer, (3) For Entry Of Default Against Defendant, And (4) For Monetary Sanctions Filed On August 9, 2019, Or, In The Alternative Terminating Sanctions, Striking Answer, Entry Of Default And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 23, 2019 line . PLAINTIFF UMPQUA BANK A STATE CHARTERED BANK's Motion For Order: (1) Compelling Further Responses To Plaintiff Umpqua Banks: (A) Demand For Inspection And Production Of Documents (Set One), And (B) Form Interrogatories (Set One) On Shortened Time Pursuant To This Courts Order Granting Ex Parte Application For Order On Motion For: (1) Terminating Sanctions, (2) Striking Defendants Answer, (3) For Entry Of Default Against Defendant, And (4) For Monetary Sanctions Filed On August 9, 2019, Or, In The Alternative Terminating Sanctions, Striking Answer, Entry Of Default And For Monetary Sanctions GRANTED. On August 9, 2019 this Court provided the Defendant another opportunity to fully comply with discovery obligations. This motion arises out of Defendant's failure to do so. No declaration of compliance as required by August 9, 2019 Order was filed and no opposition to this motion (as required and authorized by the same Order) was filed. Defendant's Answer is stricken and monetary sanctions are granted as requested in the amount of $6,060 in addition to the sanctions granted (and stayed) by August 9, 2019 Order. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SAN DONG BEST INC. ET AL VS. GENERAL TSO KITCHEN, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants General Tso Kitchen, Inc., And Li Cao'S Renotice Of Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs / Cross-Defendants San Dong Best Inc. And Shubo Wang And Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 23, 2019 line . Defendants General Tso Kitchen, Inc., And Li Cao'S Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs / Cross-Defendants San Dong Best Inc. And Shubo Wang And Counsel DENIED without prejudice. The motion was premature. Also moving party failed to show at this time that the pleadings are in violation of CCP 128.7. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SAN DONG BEST INC. ET AL VS. GENERAL TSO KITCHEN, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 23, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANTs GENERAL TSO KITCHEN, INC. and LI CAO's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to explain the inconsistences (omissions/additions) between the original and first amended complaint. Specifically, Plaintiff is to allege the terms of the entirety of the contract for the transaction at issue in this case and explain why not all of the alleged terms of the alleged transaction have been alleged if Plaintiff can do so in good faith. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SAN DONG BEST INC. ET AL VS. GENERAL TSO KITCHEN, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 23, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANT DAVID TSAO's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend for plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of each cause of action, breach of contract and violation of CC 1950.8.= (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: DEFU LU ET AL VS. RIU JUN LU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 23, 2019 line 5. PLAINTIFFs DEFU LU and GUNG-MEI CHAO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Motion For Summary Adjudication continued to August 29, 2019 for Defendant to file proper evidentiary support to his opposition. Court's Order on Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication is not evidence. Defendant may also file an amended response to the Separate Statement. Defendant's evidence shall be filed and served no later than August 26, 2019 by 2:00 p.m. Plaintiff may file a supplemental reply addressing the evidence only by no later than August 28, 2019 by 9:00 a.m. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: TED O VS. RAYNE LARDIE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 23, 2019 line 5. DEFENDANTs RAYNE LARDIE, JOSEPH POWELL, SHYLA JACKSON, and LEROY JACKSON's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT continued to August 30, 2019 to be heard by the Honorable Mary Wiss in Department 305 at 9:30 a.m. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JOSHUA CAROUBA VS. KATHRYN REED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 23, 2019 line 6. DEFENDANT KATHRYN REED's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to the second cause of action. Plaintiff shall have 5 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint alleging in good faith facts (not conclusions) showing force, menace, and/or threats of violence. If Plaintiff fails to file a timely amended complaint, Defendant shall file an answer to complaint (remaining parts) within 10 days of notice of entry of order. = (501/CFH)
